title: Which Agent Platform Is Right for You?
subtitle: OpenClaw, Claude Code, Cursor, Replit, v0 — honest comparison with no affiliate deals. Pick the right tool for your level.

sections.heading: Why This Page Exists
sections.content: There are now dozens of platforms where you can work with AI agents. Most comparison articles are sponsored. Most 'top 10' lists are ads. This page is different. No affiliate links. No sponsorships. Just honest notes from people who actually use these tools every day.

sections.heading: OpenClaw
sections.content: A full agent platform. Your AI agent lives here — with tools, memory, skills, and the ability to do real work. Best for non-technical builders who want an agent that does real work. Free tier available. The most complete agent platform right now.

sections.heading: Claude Code
sections.content: A terminal-based coding agent by Anthropic. It reads your codebase, writes code, runs commands, deploys projects. Best for developers and people learning to code. The best coding agent available right now — understands entire codebases, not just single files.

sections.heading: Cursor
sections.content: An AI-powered code editor built on VS Code with AI built in — autocomplete, chat, code generation. Best for people who want to code but prefer a visual editor over a terminal. Easier to start with than Claude Code if you're new to coding.

sections.heading: Replit
sections.content: A browser-based coding environment with AI built in. No downloads, no setup. Best for quick prototypes, learning to code, and anyone who doesn't want to install anything. The fastest path from zero to 'I built something'.

sections.heading: v0.dev
sections.content: Vercel's AI tool that generates web interfaces from text descriptions. Best for generating UI components quickly — landing pages, dashboards, forms. Amazing for first drafts of any UI fast, but not a full agent.

sections.heading: ChatGPT / Claude.ai / Gemini
sections.content: Chat interfaces to AI models. The simplest way to use AI — no setup, no tools, no learning curve. Claude.ai is best for careful, nuanced work. ChatGPT is the most popular all-rounder. Gemini is free with Google account. These are not agent platforms — they're the front door to AI.

sections.heading: How to Choose
sections.content: Never used AI? Start with ChatGPT or Claude.ai. Want an agent that does real work? OpenClaw. Want to build software? Claude Code or Cursor. Want to build fast, no install? Replit or v0.dev. Most power users end up using 2-3 of these together.

key_takeaways: Match the platform to what you want to do right now — don't overthink it
OpenClaw for agents, Claude Code for coding, chatbots for getting started
Most power users end up using 2-3 platforms together — they're tools, not religions
